MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556

HDR

High Dynamic Range (HDR) is a high dynamic range technique used in imaging and photography to reproduce a greater dynamic range of luminosity than is possible with standard digital imaging or photographic techniques. The aim is to present a similar range of luminance to that experienced through the human visual system. It expands the brightness range of the display, showing more bright and dark details and bringing richer colors and more vivid and natural detail representations to the picture, making the picture closer to what the human eye sees. HDR display technology is an upgrade of pixel quality, resulting in obvious improvement on picture quality.

Two features of HDR are as follows:

  1. High contrast
  2. Excellent color expression

Hardware Requirements

  • Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus
  • Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus LT
  • Pro Capture Dual HDMI 4K Plus LT
  • Pro Capture AIO 4K Plus

These four Pro capture devices can support HDR features and currently only the HDR10 standards.

HDR Capture and Render

Use the APIs of Pro Capture Family Functions to capture videos since rendering HDR10 images must use a 10-bit depth page-fourcc. Use the APIs of Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions to get the HDR meta data for HDR10 rendering. For details, refer to HDRCapture C++.

Capturing, Rendering, and Recording HDR Videos

SDKv3 is capable of capturing and recording videos in HDR10, encoding the video into H.265 streams with HDR data and finally saving the tracks as MP4 or MOV files.

HDR metadata is required for rendering and encoding videos. It is contained in the HDMI InfoFrame, which applies to HDMI2.0a standard when being sent to a receiver, such as a capture tool or a display device.

HDR10 uses REC2020 color space, 10 bit depth and ST2084 (PQ) transfer function. Compared with 8 bits color space, it contains more data about luminance or radiance. Magewell uses P010 to ensure that all HDR information of images remains undamaged.
When displaying HDR video, HDR10 metadata is used to set the display effect. For display device without support of HDR, SDKv3 supports mapping HDR to SDR and and thus allowing previewing HDR content in SDR mode. The HDR metadata can be obtianed using interfaces of Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions.

To support HDR10 metadata for High Dynamic Range (HDR), HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding, H.265) is used to compress a video into a HDR file sampling in 10bit.

HDRCapture C++ demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture HDR10 videos using Pro Capture Family Functions, render using OpenGL, and record the data into MP4 files using MP4.
